search_terms_used: heat stress running performance systematic review meta-analysis
hot weather marathon performance research paper
heat acclimatization endurance running protocol systematic review
cold weather running performance research endurance
WBGT running performance guidelines research paper
heat training adaptation endurance athletes temperate conditions performance
coverage_notes: Covers the landmark weather-marathon paper (Ely 2007, already noted in manifest), an updated large-scale weather analysis across 1258 races (Mantzios 2022), the definitive comprehensive review on exercise under heat stress (Periard 2021), the expert consensus on training/competing in heat (Racinais 2015), a meta-analysis of heat adaptation effects on performance (Tyler 2016), heat acclimation decay and re-induction timing (Daanen 2018), a practical heat acclimation protocol using hot water immersion (Zurawlew 2016), and a comprehensive cold environment review (Castellani & Tipton 2016). Also included the Lorenzo 2010 landmark RCT showing heat acclimation improves cool-condition performance. Gap: no dedicated wind/rain adjustment paper was found with sufficient quality — wind effects are partially captured in Mantzios 2022. Cold running research is sparse compared to heat; Castellani 2016 covers the broadest ground but is not running-specific.

papers.title: Impact of weather on marathon-running performance
papers.notes: Landmark study quantifying the impact of WBGT on marathon performance. Found progressive slowing as WBGT increases from 5 to 25C, with slower runners more affected than faster runners. Established that performance degradation is proportional to thermal stress and varies by ability level. Essential reference for pace adjustment calculators in hot conditions.

papers.title: Effects of Weather Parameters on Endurance Running Performance: Discipline-specific Analysis of 1258 Races
papers.notes: Largest analysis of weather on running performance: 1258 races (1936-2019) across 42 countries covering marathon, 50km racewalk, 20km racewalk, 10000m, 5000m, and steeplechase. Found optimal WBGT of 7.5-15C (air temp 10-17.5C), with 0.3-0.4% performance decline per degree outside optimum. WBGT had better predictive power than air temperature alone (R2=0.11-0.47 vs 0.04-0.34). Provides discipline-specific adjustment factors.

papers.title: Exercise under heat stress: thermoregulation, hydration, performance implications, and mitigation strategies
papers.notes: The definitive modern comprehensive review (107 pages) on exercise in heat. Covers thermoregulation mechanisms, cardiovascular strain, CNS fatigue, dehydration effects, heat acclimation protocols, cooling strategies, and hydration guidelines. Essential single reference for understanding the full scope of heat-exercise interactions. Supersedes older reviews with updated evidence through 2020.

papers.title: Consensus recommendations on training and competing in the heat
papers.journal: Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports
papers.notes: Expert consensus from 17 leading researchers providing actionable recommendations for athletes training/competing in heat. Key guidance: 1-2 weeks heat acclimatization with repeated exercise-heat exposures, start competition euhydrated, minimize dehydration, use pre-cooling and per-cooling strategies. The go-to practical reference for coaches preparing athletes for hot-weather events.

papers.title: The Effects of Heat Adaptation on Physiology, Perception and Exercise Performance in the Heat: A Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analysis quantifying heat adaptation benefits: improved exercise performance/capacity (g=0.7), reduced resting core temperature (g=-0.6), reduced heart rate (g=-0.5). Found medium-term protocols (8-14 days) most common; long-term HA showed highest effect sizes. Provides pooled evidence that heat adaptation significantly and meaningfully improves performance in the heat, with specific effect sizes coaches can use to justify the investment.

papers.title: Heat Acclimation Decay and Re-Induction: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
papers.notes: Critical for heat acclimation programming: quantified that HR and core temperature adaptations decay at ~2.5% per day without heat exposure. After 2 weeks without heat exposure, end-exercise HR decayed ~35%, sweat rate ~30%, and core temp ~6%. Full acclimation lost within a month. Re-induction possible with 2-4 days of exercise-heat training. Essential for timing heat acclimation blocks relative to target races.

papers.title: Heat acclimation improves exercise performance
papers.notes: Landmark RCT showing 10 days of heat acclimation (40C) improved 1-hour time trial performance by 5-8% in BOTH hot AND cool (13C) conditions vs control group. Also increased VO2max, lactate threshold, and cardiac output. Demonstrated that heat acclimation is not just for hot races — it has ergogenic effects even in temperate conditions, making it a legitimate training tool year-round. N=20 (12 heat, 8 control).

papers.title: Cold Stress Effects on Exposure Tolerance and Exercise Performance
papers.notes: The most comprehensive review on cold and exercise performance. Covers cold shock responses, hypothermia physiology, and performance effects. Found aerobic performance is degraded in cold environments through decreased muscle temperature, impaired cardiovascular function, increased carbohydrate reliance, and higher lactate production. Skin cooling alone reduces endurance by ~30%, with core cooling adding another 30-40%. Essential for understanding why pacing needs adjustment in cold as well as heat.
